concerts.label: A Jazz Radio Benefit featuring Bob James and Steps
Mika Yoshida, Marimba, and Richard Stoltzman, Clarinet

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been playing with him for 46 years now... That was more Mike’s baby... We were a barn band... Mike Mainieri on vibes, and I played Rhodes
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: For about two and a half years, we had Mike Mainieri and a few other people and Buddy. And that went very well. We just traveled around the world because he had a State Department tour
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: Mike and I got together and rehearsed a lot of complicated lines... getting into the solo work and the duo work with Mike.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: One wedding reception that I played in Queens, subbing for Mike Mainieri, who couldn't make the gig.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: Mike Mainieri, first record. Selden Powell, Earl May, McKenna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
